summary: Next.js App Router's architecture is built around Server Components by default, with Client Components as opt-in boundaries. Following this model minimizes bundle size, improves performance, and simplifies data fetching.
reasoning: Server Components execute only on the server, sending zero JavaScript to the client, which reduces bundle size and improves Time to Interactive
Fetching data in Server Components eliminates client-server waterfalls and removes the need for loading spinners caused by useEffect patterns
Server Actions provide built-in progressive enhancement, CSRF protection, and a type-safe RPC-like interface for mutations
The App Router's file conventions (loading.tsx, error.tsx) provide automatic Suspense and Error Boundary integration
